Petition For Order Granting The Executor Authority To Sell Real Estate 1 Under Full Powers An Authority Under The Independent Administration Of Estates Act (Probate Code 10450) Or 2 To Enter A Listing Agreement With Sfrealestate.Com
Off Calendar Oct-19-2022 Advanced From Jan-25-23 To Dec-14-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Filing Proof Of Additonal Bond
Off Calendar Jan-17-2023 Continued To Jan-31-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance via Zoom: Roger Gross, Esq. for Phillip A. Walker (Petitioner).

The Court stated that that matter was on for status on the filing of proof of the additional bond, that Mr. Gross had asked the Court to advance the matter due to some urgency, that the court allowed it, and that now the Court is expected to hurry up and wait. Mr. Gross stated that he respected the Court's comments, that he contacted the bondsman immediately, that he could not file until he had received the signed bond from his client, that he just received the signed bond at the beginning of last week, that this is a recurring issue, and that he can express the Court's displeasure to his client.

At the conclusion of Mr. Gross' comments, the Court continued the status on filing proof of additional bond to January 31, 2023, at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204. If proof is not on file by January 26, 2023, Mr. Gross and his client are ordered to appear. Mr. Gross noted that he had submitted the bond for filing, that it was rejected due to the lack of his accompanying declaration, that he was unaware of that requirement, and that it was corrected and promptly filed on Friday.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: Choi. Matter not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Filing Proof Of Additonal Bond
Off Calendar Jan-10-2023 Continued To Jan-17-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Order Granting The Executor Authority To Sell Real Estate 1 Under Full Powers An Authority Under The Independent Administration Of Estates Act (Probate Code 10450) Or 2 To Enter A Listing Agreement With Sfrealestate.Com
Off Calendar Dec-16-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Roger Gross, via Zoom, for and with Phillip A. Walker, Personal Representative.

At the court's query, counsel reports as to why he is filing the instant petition now and the status of the Estate's real property which required extensive repairs to prepare it for sale. Counsel advises that full IAEA authority was not requested in the initial petition because the plan was to keep the property and that full IAEA authority is sought in the instant petition to provide liquidity of assets to make pecuniary gifts and pay for estate expenses. The marketing plan for the sale of the real property is discussed as is the plan regarding closing the estate as soon as the sale of the real property is closed. The court and counsel discuss the bond issue with counsel advising that he is working on obtaining consents regarding waiver of bond.

After additional discussion, the matter is taken under submission. The Status on Close of Estate currently set on Feb-06-2023 is continued in open court to Jun-12-2023 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Cristobal. Not Reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Sep-08-2021 Continued To Nov-17-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Roger Gross, via ZOOM, for Phillip Walker, via ZOOM, Executor.

The court begins by reminding Mr. Gross that at the previous calling of this matter he had advised the court that he would have a petition ready to file within two (2) weeks. Mr. Gross reports that he had submitted all of the documents that he had to his paralegal, but that the paralegal had determined that there were missing documents and that he is waiting for Mr. Walker to submit those missing documents to him. Mr. Walker then confirms that Charles Schwab had sent documents to him but that he then subsequently realized that there were missing and advised Charles Schwab, but that they have yet to reply. Mr. Walker then advises that he intends to contact Charles Schwab to request the missing documents and work with Mr. Gross to complete the accounting. The court then instructs Mr. Gross to file a new Status Petition. The court then advises Mr. Gross that if a Petition for Approval of Status of Administration and Accounting Petition are not filed by the next calling of this case, the court will consider suspending the Administrator. Additionally, if the two (2) petitions requested by the court are not filed by 11/03/2021, the court will require appearances from both Mr. Gross and Mr. Walker.

The Status on Close of Estate and Status on Filing of First Accounting are continued to 11/17/2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

Ex Parte Petition To Advance Hearing Date For Petition For Authority To Borrow Money And To Execute Security Agreement

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Jul-28-2021 Continued To Sep-08-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Roger Gross, via ZOOM, for Phillip Walker, Executor.

The court begins by confirming with counsel of the document submitted for filing and is pending in the court's e-filing queue. Counsel reports that he has had issues lately in with e-filing and indicated in his most recent submission that it was time sensitive. The court advises counsel that it recognizes the current issues with delays in e-filing documents but confirms that the court is working on resolving the delays caused by staffing shortages. Mr. Gross then reports that all involved in this matter agree with the petition that is currently pending.

The Status on Close of Estate and Status on Filing of First Accounting are continued to September 8, 2021, at 9:00 am in Department 204.

Judge: Hon. Mary Wiss. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: B. Tso. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of The First Accounting
Off Calendar Jun-30-2021 Continued To Jul-28-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Jun-30-2021 Continued To Jul-28-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Roger Gross appeared by zoom for and with Phillip Walker by zoom.

The Court noted that there is no petition for final distribution, or a petition for approval of report on status of administration on file, and that they are now due for an accounting. Mr. Gross stated that all of the materials were delivered to the Paralegal for preparation of the accounting, that the Paralegal fell behind and went on vacation, that he has brought in another Paralegal and that he expects to have it filed within the next two weeks. Mr. Gross also stated that the accounting will be through December 31, 2020, that he has a current status report on file with the Court and that the estate is not prepared to close because of a creditor's claim by one of the tenants. The Court informed Mr. Gross that the status report he spoke was taken off calendar because it was inadequate. Status on close of estate, and status on filing of the first accounting are continued to July 28, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. If both petitions are filed by July 21, 2021, there is no need for Mr. Gross to appear.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: D. Johnson. Not reported.

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar May-19-2021 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Roger Gross appeared by zoom for Phillip Walker.

Mr. Gross confirmed that Michael Garcia was not served and stated that he thought everyone had been served, that it was an error and that he did not receive the Probate Examiner's notes. The Court noted that the tentative ruling for today referred to an earlier tentative ruling, that this issue has been outstanding for quite a while and reminded Mr. Gross that he has to be careful and make sure he is getting the Examiner's notes. The Court also noted that a two-year accounting is coming up and stated that Mr. Gross needs to think about having a discussion with his Client as to whether to take the house pre-sale, or post-sale and noted that Mr. Walker will not have a choice. Mr. Gross stated that he has advised his Client to start getting ready for the accounting. Mr. Gross also stated that the house must be sold because the estate is without cash at this point, that one tenant has not been paying rent and that in order to sell the house, his Client has been instructed to file a petition to borrow money on a bridge note to make repairs and get the property in a position to be sold. In addition, Mr. Gross stated that he will be filing a petition to borrow money and a petition to sell the house. The Petition for Approval of Report on Status of Administration is off calendar. Status on close of estate is set for June 30, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Status on filing of the first account is also set for June 30, 2021. Mr. Gross is to file his petition by June 23, 2021.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Not reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Mar-19-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Roger Gross for Petitioner.

The Court noted that there is an issue regarding the bond amount. Mr. Gross stated that the major asset is real estate, that he does not anticipate the property being sold, that there is a mortgage of about $240,000.00, that the property pulls in $62,000.00 a year in rental income and that there may be $50,000.00 in liquid assets. The Court granted the Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary with limited authority (subject to review of the order). Bond in the amount of $113,000.00 is required. Status on close of estate is set for September 14, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.
